site.title: BitAiir
site.description: A decentralized payment cryptocurrency — fast, zero-fee, and mineable by anyone.

footer.tagline: A decentralized payment cryptocurrency — fast, zero-fee, and mineable by anyone.
footer.legal: The BitAiir Developers. Released under the MIT license.

hero.title: Decentralized money that moves in seconds.
hero.subtitle: BitAiir is an open, decentralized payment cryptocurrency. Zero fees, 5-second confirmations, and a proof-of-work designed to keep mining accessible on any ordinary computer.

features.title: Built for spending, not speculation.
features.subtitle: Four decisions shape the way BitAiir feels every day.

fast.title: Confirmations in seconds
fast.body: A 5-second block time means payments settle while you're still looking at the screen. Wallets can treat one confirmation as final for everyday amounts.

zero_fees.title: Zero transaction fees
zero_fees.body: No mandatory fee. Anti-spam comes from a tiny proof of work on each transaction — about two seconds of CPU from the sender — so honest users are never priced out.

cpu_mineable.title: Mineable on any computer
cpu_mineable.body: Proof of Aiir wraps SHA-256d in Argon2id (64 MiB memory-hard). The memory cost kneecaps ASICs and GPUs, keeping regular CPUs competitive.

decentralized.title: No pre-mine, no foundation cut
decentralized.body: Every coin enters circulation through a mined block. Issuance halves over time but never stops — a permanent 10 AIIR tail keeps miners paid forever.

how.title: A familiar model, tuned for payments.
how.subtitle: BitAiir keeps what works in Bitcoin and changes only what gets in the way of everyday use.

utxo.title: UTXO accounting
utxo.body: Balances are derived from unspent outputs, not a global ledger. No smart-contract surface, no shared mutable state — just signed transfers.

poa.title: Proof of Aiir
poa.body: Blocks are secured by a double-SHA-256 hash wrapped in a 64 MiB memory-hard Argon2id step. The result is a mining race where commodity silicon wins.

p2p.title: Peer-to-peer by default
p2p.body: Nodes gossip transactions and blocks directly. There are no trusted indexers, no sequencers, and no custodial bridges in the protocol.

terminal.title: A full node in a single terminal.
terminal.body: Launch the daemon in interactive mode and mine, send, and inspect the chain from a single TUI — no extra tooling required.
terminal.caption: BitAiir Core — interactive mode

cta.title: Ready to try it?
cta.body: BitAiir is open source and pre-mainnet. Grab the latest build, or read the protocol to see how each decision is justified.

hub.title: Learn how BitAiir works.
hub.subtitle: Protocol reference, quickstart guides, and operational recipes for running the BitAiir software.

protocol.title: Protocol specification
protocol.description: The consensus rules of BitAiir — constants, transactions, blocks, Proof of Aiir, and everything else a competing implementation must match.

coming_soon.title: Quickstart & RPC
coming_soon.description: Step-by-step onboarding and a reference for every JSON-RPC method. Coming in the next iteration of the site.

translation.banner_body: This page hasn't been translated yet. The English content is shown below.

download.title: Get BitAiir running on your machine.
download.subtitle: BitAiir is pre-mainnet — pre-built binaries land with the first mainnet release. For now, build from source. The Rust toolchain is the only prerequisite.

build.title: Build from source
build.body: Works on Linux, macOS, and Windows. Five minutes plus the time `cargo` takes to fetch and compile dependencies.
build.step1_body: Use rustup. The toolchain pinned in the repo is automatically picked up by `rust-toolchain.toml` — no manual version selection needed.
build.step1_windows_note: On Windows, install Rust from rustup.rs. The installer pulls in the MSVC build tools.
build.step2_body: The `--release` flag is important — debug builds are 5-10× slower at mining.
build.step2_cmd:
git clone https://github.com/bitaiir/bitaiir.git
cd bitaiir
cargo build --release
build.step3_body: Launch in interactive mode for the TUI:
build.windows_callout: On Windows, executables get the `.exe` extension and paths use backslashes.

install.title: Install globally with cargo
install.body: Optional — gets `bitaiird` and `bitaiir-cli` on your `PATH` so you can call them from anywhere.
install.cmd:
cargo install --path crates/bitaiir-daemon
cargo install --path crates/bitaiir-cli

binaries.title: Pre-built binaries
binaries.body: We will publish signed binaries on GitHub Releases when mainnet ships.

next.title: What now?
next.body: If this is your first time, follow the quickstart to get from a clean clone to your first mined block in under ten minutes.

quickstart.title: From zero to your first block.
quickstart.subtitle: Six commands. One terminal. By the end you'll have a running node, a wallet address, and the ability to mine and send AIIR.
quickstart.step1_body: BitAiir is written in Rust (edition 2024). Install the toolchain with rustup; everything else is handled automatically.
quickstart.step1_windows_note: On Windows, download the installer from rustup.rs — it bundles the MSVC build tools.
quickstart.step2_body: The release build takes a few minutes the first time around — `cargo` caches everything for the next run.
quickstart.step2_cmd:
git clone https://github.com/bitaiir/bitaiir.git
cd bitaiir
cargo build --release
quickstart.step3_body: Run in interactive mode to get the TUI. The daemon does not mine by default.
quickstart.step4_body: Inside the TUI, type the slash command. Block subsidy is 100 AIIR; on a modern laptop a block lands every few seconds.
quickstart.step5_title: Generate an address and send a payment
quickstart.step5_body: Each address starts empty — mining adds AIIR to the wallet's most recent address.
quickstart.step6_body: When you're done, shut the daemon down so it can flush state and remove the cookie file.

rpc.title: Every command the daemon understands.
rpc.subtitle: JSON-RPC methods exposed by `bitaiird` over HTTP, mirrored as `bitaiir-cli` subcommands. Authentication is cookie-based by default — see the core README for LAN-style user/password setup.

community.title: Talk to the BitAiir builders.
community.subtitle: BitAiir is volunteer-run and open source. Everything happens in the open — code reviews, design discussions, support questions. Pick the channel that fits.

github.title: GitHub
github.body: Source code, issues, discussions, pull requests. The canonical home of every conversation that turns into code.

twitter.title: X (Twitter)
twitter.body: Release announcements, milestones, and short-form updates. Lightweight follow for status without the firehose.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.body: Real-time chat with the community. Best place for quick questions and informal discussion.

contact.title: General contact
contact.body: For partnerships, press, or anything that doesn't fit the public channels above.

security.title: Security disclosure
security.body: Found a vulnerability? Please report it privately first. The core repo's SECURITY.md has full disclosure guidelines.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.body: New contributors welcome. Read the core repo's CONTRIBUTING.md for the workflow — DCO sign-off, conventional commits, PR templates.
